title: Foster Care/Temporary Shelter for Animals - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Foster Care/Temporary Shelter for Animals 6 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ARRAY ANIMAL RESCUE ANIMAL RESCUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides foster services for domestic pets. 6426 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness, substance abuse, and domestic violence Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 851-4459 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cat and dog services including adoption, foster and volunteer opportunities, an events calendar, and pet resources. Accepts donations from their wish list page as well as those of a monetary nature. 1875 Highway 99 N Suite B-10 Ashland, OR 975359752 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Ashland office phone: Tuesday-Friday noon-4pm Donation drop off: Monday 1pm-4pm Email (541) 708-0090 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides animal adoptions; reunification services; emergency boarding in crisis situations such as domestic violence, medical emergencies, disaster response, and other situations determined to be such; spay/neuter services for feral cats; a pet food pantry; foster care; humane education; dog licenses; and volunteer opportunities. 88530 Green Hill Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 11am-6pm Email (541) 689-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-door admission, low-cost adoptions, Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) for feral cats, and working cat adoptions for shelter animals. Offers lost and found pet assistance, a pet food pantry, low-cost vaccine clinics, a safe housing program for pets of people in crisis, and low-cost end-of-life services for the community. 398 SW Twin Oaks Circle Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday noon-6pm, Saturday-Sunday noon-5pm Email (541) 757-9000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER SHELTERING ANIMALS AND FAMILIES TOGETHER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe place to stay for the pets of eligible individuals. Animals allowed to stay in shelter with victims when space is available. PO Box 1358 Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Pets of individuals who are survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, dating violence, or stalking Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 850-8939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PAWS ANIMAL SHELTER ANIMAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a no-kill animal shelter specializing in care for orphaned, homeless, and newborn animals. Animals are screened for diseases on intake. Offers adoption and foster opportunities. The shelter has limited capacity and services may not always be available. In these instances, shelter staff may still be able to provide information or assist in directing to other available services in the community. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 650-0855 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARRAY ANIMAL RESCUE ANIMAL RESCUE FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER SHELTERING ANIMALS AND FAMILIES TOGETHER
